name: Skill Seekers
what_it_does: A universal preprocessing platform that converts documentation websites, GitHub repositories, and PDFs into structured AI knowledge assets, exportable to 16+ formats including Claude Skills, Gemini Skills, OpenAI GPTs, and vector database formats.
use_cases: Convert any documentation site or GitHub repo into a Claude skill ZIP in minutes instead of days of manual work
Generate RAG-ready pre-chunked documents with metadata for Chroma, FAISS, or Qdrant vector databases
Create context files for IDE coding assistants (Cursor, Windsurf, Continue.dev) from framework documentation
Build multi-platform AI skills from a single processed asset targeting Claude, Gemini, and OpenAI simultaneously
Analyze GitHub repos for conflicts between documented APIs and actual code implementations via AST parsing
not_for: Teams needing real-time or live-updating knowledge bases — processing is batch-based
Scenarios requiring deep semantic understanding beyond chunking and metadata extraction
Projects where content licensing prohibits automated scraping and redistribution
best_when: You need to rapidly onboard AI assistants to a specific framework, library, or internal codebase by creating high-quality structured knowledge assets from existing documentation.
avoid_when: Your knowledge sources are already well-structured for AI consumption or your target platform has a native documentation import feature.

auth.notes: Requires LLM API keys for AI enhancement features: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GOOGLE_API_KEY, or OPENAI_API_KEY depending on target platform. Core scraping and processing works without keys.

pricing.notes: MIT open source. AI enhancement features incur LLM API costs proportional to documentation size processed.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: CLI tool with standard Python error output; MCP server error handling not specifically documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Processing the same source twice produces the same output if source content hasn't changed, but no explicit idempotency guarantees.
known_agent_gotchas: Version 3.1.0-dev indicates the package is still in active development with potential breaking changes
OCR for scanned PDFs requires additional system dependencies not bundled with pip install
GitHub API rate limits apply during repo analysis; large repos may fail without token configuration
The 'automatic conflict detection' feature between docs and code is novel but accuracy is unverified
10k star count may be inflated given the dev version status — verify community maturity before production use

security_score_components.security_notes: Community/specialized tool. Apply standard security practices for category. Review documentation for specific security requirements.
